description: The bīt Kidmuri is one of the temples located in the citadel of Nineveh. Its Sumerian ceremonial name is not preserved in extant cuneiform sources. Although the temple's exact location remains unknown, it is generally believed to have been between the temple of the goddess Ištar (Emašmaš) and the Citadel Gate (East Gate).

name: Kidmuri temple (Kalhu)
description: When Kalhu (biblical Calah, modern Nimrud) became the principal administrative center of the Assyrian Empire in the ninth century BC, king Ashurnasirpal II (r. 883–859 BC) built (or rebuilt) numerous temples. One of those was dedicated to the divine Kidmuri (probably a collective name for a group of gods).

name: Kiosk of Qertassi
description: The so-called Kiosk of Qertassi is an unfinished Roman kiosk that may date to the reign of Trajan. Originally located at Qertassi, the remains of the temple were relocated to New Kalabsha in Southern Egypt as part of the International Campaign to Save the Monuments of Nubia in the twentieth century.

name: Kiratah
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C5 Kiratah

name: Kirche auf der Akropolis
description: A Byzantine church located within the fortified acropolis of Aperlae.

name: Kition
description: Kition was an ancient city founded on the southern coast of Cyprus in the thirteenth century B.C.

name: Klaros
description: The site of a temple of Apollo administered by Colophonians and active from the seventh century B.C. to the fourth century A.D.

name: Kleonai
description: An ancient city located in the mountainous area southwest of Corinth. The acropolis of ancient Kleonai lies about two kilometers east of the modern town named after it in the 1960s (Archaies Kleones, formerly Kontostavlos).

name: Knowlton Church and Earth Works
description: A cluster of Neolithic and Bronze Age earthworks with a twelfth-century Norman church built in the center.

name: Kobosh
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 81 C2 Kobosh

name: Koçak
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 D3 Koçak

name: Koh Ker
description: An ancient temple complex of the Khmer Empire, Koh Ker is also known as Lingapura or Chok Gargyar. The site is located in the Srayong commune of Preah, Vihear province's Kulen district. The site was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage site in September 2023 and covers some 1,187 ha.

name: Koi Krylgan Kala
description: Koi Krylgan Kala is an archaeological site in Uzbekistan. At the site are the remains of a temple complex associated with the Chorasmian Dynasty, which was destroyed approximately 200 BC.

name: Kolona
description: An archaeological site on the island of Aegina that shows multiple settlement phases from the Bronze Age to the Classical period.

name: Kom el-Nana
description: Area south of the main city of Amarna that was the site of a temple during the Amarna period. The structure was overbuilt by a Christian monastery in late antiquity.

name: Komissariato
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Komissariato

name: Komosarue
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 M2 Komosarue

name: Kordin III
description: Megalithic temple in Malta constructed during the Mġarr phase (ca. 3700 BCE) and abandoned ca. 2500 BCE.

name: Kordin Temples
description: A group of megalithic temples on Malta constructed during the Neolithic period.

name: Korykion Antron (Parnassos)
description: A sacred cave on mount Parnassos named for Apollo's daughter Corycia. It is not to be confused with another cave of the same name at Corycus in Cilicia.

name: Köşkerli
description: Modern name for ruins of a Byzantine church and surrounding village in eastern Rough Cilicia

name: Kosmas kai Damianos, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 53 A2 Kosmas kai Damianos, Mon.

name: Kothon
description: The so-called "Kothon" is a large, rectangular, artificial pool dating to the Punic period of Sicily and located on the island of Motya. Originally interpreted as an artificial port basin, recent research reinterpets it as a freshwater basin with functions sacred to the god Ba'al and constituting the central component of a large sanctuary.

name: Koustilya
description: A late-antique rural church now lacking decoration located about 6km north of the modern village of Tozeur in Tunisia.

name: Kranaion Antron
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 C2 Kranaion Antron

name: Kraneia?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 D3 Kraneia?

name: Kraneion Early Christian Basilica
description: An Early Christian Basilica located near the grove of Kraneion that dates to the sixth century CE.

name: Krounoi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 B4 Krounoi

name: Ksar Djaria
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 34 F2 Ksar Djaria

name: Kubaniya
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 inset Kubaniya

name: Kuh-i Ayyub
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C3 Kuh-i Ayyub

name: Kuh-i Shahrak
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C3 Kuh-i Shahrak

name: Kunar Siah
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C5 Kunar Siah

name: Kuntillat 'Ajrud
description: A late ninth to early eighth century BC site located in the northeastern part of the Sinai peninsula, the fortress-like Kuntillat ‘Ajrud may have been a sanctuary. Hebrew and Phoenician inscriptions have been identified at the site.

name: Kursi paleochristian basilica
description: An early Christian basilica was excavated at Kursi, near the Sea of Galilee in the Golan Heights.

name: Kushk Khainfar
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 B3 Kushk Khainfar

name: Kybele Sanctuary at Kapikaya
description: The Kybele Sanctuary at Kapikaya is a rock-cut sanctuary sacred to Kybele, approximately 6 km from Pergamon, and is included in the UNESCO World Heritage site "Pergamon and its Multi-Layered Cultural Landscape".

name: Kynosarges
description: A gymnasium and sanctuary of Herakles at Athens, located southeast of the Acropolis

name: L’Acapte
description: Site of a rural sanctuary dedicated to Aristaeus, son of Apollo.

name: La Bauche
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 F3 La Bauche

name: La Cuma, Monte Rinaldo
description: The principal archaeological remains found in the "La Cuma, Monte Rinaldo" site are those of a late Republican sanctuary dating to the third through first centuries B.C.

name: La Motte-St-Martin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 F4 La Motte-St-Martin

name: La Temple de la Madeleine, Geneva
description: The oldest Christian church in the city of Geneva, the Temple de la Madeleine site has been in continuous use as a church since the 5th century. The current structure on the site dates to the 15th century.

name: Labraunda
description: Labraunda is an archaeological site located in Caria, just west of Ortaköy, Turkey. The site was sacred to both Carians and Mysians.

name: Labyrinthos
description: Labyrinthos is a name applied by Greek authors, notably Herodotus and Strabo, to describe a monumental funerary temple at Hawara.

name: Lachau
description: A Gallo-Roman sanctuary.

name: Lacus Iuturnae
description: The Lacus Iuturnae was a pool built near a spring sacred to Iuturna. Legend held that the Dioscuri watered their horses there after the Battle of Lake Regillus in 495 BCE.

name: Lamyatt
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 E3 Lamyatt

name: Laneia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 Laneia

name: Lang-i Rud
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 G2 Lang-i Rud

name: Large Temple at Sikait
description: A large rock-cut temple at Sikait in Egypt's Eastern desert.

name: Largo di Torre Argentina
description: A square in the Campus Martius of Rome, at the center of which lies an archaeological site that includes the remains of four temples of the Republican period, as well as elements of the first century B.C. theater complex of Cnaeus Pompeius.

name: Late Mithraeum
description: Final phase of a sacred space dedicated to the worship of the god Mithras at Dura-Europos.

name: Late Roman Round Building
description: A round building constructed in the northwestern corner of the Athenian Agora in the 5th century AD over the Crossroads Enclosure. It was ruined in the late 5th or 6th century AD.

name: Lateran Basilica, Palace, and Castra Nova equitum singularium
description: Site of a basilica and palace from early Roman imperial times, as well as an military installation built by Septimius Severus for the Roman imperial cavalry bodyguard. Now the site of the Archbasilica of St. John Lateran (rebuilt in the 16th century): Complesso dei San Giovanni in Laterano (Basilica, Palazzo Apostolico Lateranense, edifici annessi) Holy See; listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1990.

name: Latomos Monastery
description: A late fifth century AD church, later associated with a small monastery in Thessaloniki, Greece.

name: Laubendorf
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 F3 Laubendorf

name: Laveyron
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 D3 Laveyron

name: Laxia tis Skhistis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B2 Laxia tis Skhistis

name: Le Bouchet-St-Nicolas
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 B4 Le Bouchet-St-Nicolas

name: Le Ciliegeta
description: The site of Le Ciliegeta, near the source of the river Arno, served as a rich stips votiva. The site was discovered in 1836. The finds included over 600 bronzes, more than 1,000 pieces of aes rude, 2,000 weapons, and other artifacts.

name: Le Pinnacle
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 7 D2 Le Pinnacle

name: Le Serre d’Astier
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 F5 Le Serre d’Astier

name: Lehun
description: An ancient site located on the northern plateau of the Wadi Mujib in modern Jordan. Under excavation since the 1970s, the site has so far provided evidence for human activity from the prehistoric period through the Islamic.

name: Lendorf
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 F3 Lendorf

name: Lentilly
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 D2 Lentilly

name: Lera
description: An ancient cave sanctuary on the Akrotiri peninsula of Crete.

name: Les Courents
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 D2 Les Courents

name: Les Vaux de la Celle
description: An ancient Gallo-Roman sanctuary near modern Genainville (France), with excavated remains of a temple, nymphaeum, and theater: BAtlas 11 B3 Génainville (sic)

name: Letoon
description: Sanctuary of Leto and center of the Lycian federation.

name: Leuke Akte
description: Leuke Akte, the ‘White cape’ located on the Libyan coast of Egypt, was the site of an Isis cult wherein the Egyptian goddess was worshipped as Aphrodite, Muchis, and Eseremphis. An oracular shrine of Apollo was also found there.

name: Leukothea
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 88 B3 Leukothea

name: Limani Ozia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 B3 Limani Ozia

name: Lincietti
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B1 Lincietti

name: Little Hagia Sophia
description: former Greek Eastern Orthodox church in Istanbul converted to a mosque

name: Little Metropolis church (Athens)
description: The Little Metropolis church or Panagia Gorgoepikoos is a Frankish, Late Byzantine, or Ottoman-period domed cross-in-square church in central Athens. It is decorated with spolia from earlier structures.

name: Lloret
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 H4 Lloret

name: Locoteiacus
description: A Benedictine abbey that is located 7 km south of Poitiers and was founded by Martin from 361 to 371.

name: London Mithraeum
description: A temple sacred to Mithras in Londinium that was discovered in 1954. The visible remains of the temple do not sit on the original site, but have been reassembled at Temple Court, Queen Victoria Street, London.

name: Los Cuadriales
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 D3 Los Cuadriales

name: Lousoi
description: A settlement in Azania, located near modern Kato Lusi, that has been under excavation since the late 19th century.

name: Loutra Hypatis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C3 Loutra Hypatis

name: Lower Church at Banganarti
description: Church dating to the 7th century CE in Nubia that was succeeded by St. Raphael Church II. The site is located in the modern Sudanese village of Banganarti.

name: Lucus Feroniae
description: A sanctuary of Feronia located at a spring on the via Appia near Tarracina.

name: Lucus Furrinae
description: The Lucus Furrinae or Syrian Sanctuary is a sanctuary site on the Janiculum Hill in Rome. The first known construction at the site took place during the late first century CE, and it remained in use until the mid-fourth century CE.

name: Lupercal
description: A cave located at the foot of the Palatine Hill in Rome in which the she-wolf was said to have been found suckling the twins, Romulus and Remus.

name: Luxeuil Abbey
description: Located in France's Burgundy region, the Luxeuil Abbey was founded ca. 585–590 by the Irish missionary Saint Columbanus.

name: Luxor Temple
description: An ancient Egyptian temple that was initially built ca. 1400 BCE.

name: Lyceum
description: A sanctuary of Apollo Lyceios in Athens, which served as the seat of Aristotle's Peripatetic school of philosophy founded in 335/4 BCE. The Lyceum was destroyed by Sulla in 86 BCE.

name: Lydney
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 8 E3 Lydney

name: Lydney Roman temple
description: A fourth century AD temple dedicated to Nodens.

name: Lykosoura
description: A city of Arcadia and home of the cult of Despoina

name: Lysi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Lysi

name: Lysimacheia
description: An ancient city in Aetolia, located 3km south of the modern city that now bears its name.

name: Macchia delle Valli, Vetralla
description: A rock-cut Etrusco-Roman sanctuary sacred to Demeter that was discovered during rescue excavations in 2006 following looting activity. The sanctuary, set in a grove, remained in use until the second century A.D.

name: Madhukeshwara temple
description: A Shiva temple at Banavasi in India whose foundation may date as early as the 7th century CE.

name: Madonna del Parto
description: Madonna del Parto is a Christian church at Sutri, dating to the thirteenth and fourteenth centuries. The rock-cut sanctuary repurposes a Mithraeum dating to the first and second centuries.

name: Madonnna del Canneto
description: The sanctuary site of Madonnna del Canneto is located at Settefrati within the confines of the Abruzzo national park in Italy. It has a long history as a sacred site, beginning with sacred activities dedicated to Mefitis in the later first millennium BCE.

name: Magnetum?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 C3 Magnetum?

name: Magoula
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 C4 Magoula

name: Magoula Kastri
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 C1 Magoula Kastri

name: Mahabalipuram
description: This group of sanctuaries was carved out of rock along the Coromandel coast in the seventh and eighth centuries. The Group of Monuments at Mahabalipuram is a UNESCO World Heritage site.

name: Mahabodhi Temple
description: One of the four main Buddhist pilgrimage sites, identified as the place where Gautama Buddha attained Enlightenment. The first temple on the site was built by Emperor Asoka in the third century B.C., and the present temple dates from the fifth or sixth centuries. It is one of the earliest Buddhist temples built entirely in brick, still standing in India, from the late Gupta period. The site was largely abandoned from the twelfth-eighteenth centuries. It was named a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2002.

name: Maimeh
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 B3 Maimeh

name: Maison Carrée
description: A well-preserved Roman temple built ca. 16 B.C. and dedicated to Caius and Lucius Caesar.

name: Makaravank'
description: Medieval Armenian church and monastic complex dated to the 9th-13th centuries.

name: Malchittu nuragic temple
description: A sacred structure with an 'in antis' plan located near Arzachena in Sardinia.

name: Malla
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 H4 Malla

name: Mănăstirea Halmyris
description: An Early Christian basilica and martyrium at Halmyris.

name: Mandra (Despotiko)
description: A substantial multiperiod sanctuary and associated complex on the Aegean island of Despotiko. Structures and finds date from the early Iron Age through Roman times, with some evidence for use or reuse into the Byzantine period.

name: Mandra di Mezzo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 C3 Mandra di Mezzo

name: Manganello Sanctuary
description: The Manganello Sanctuary is located at the northern edge Caere's urban plateau.

name: Manglisi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 88 C3 Manglisi

name: Manna
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Manna

name: Maqam er-Rab
description: A peripteral temple built of basalt that includes an altar to Nemesis. Epigraphic data provides a chronology of late second to third centuries A.D.

name: Maqsudabad
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C4 Maqsudabad

name: Mar Abay
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 C3 Mar Abay

name: Mar Abrohom
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D3 Mar Abrohom

name: Mar Awgin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D3 Mar Awgin

name: Mar Behnam
description: The Mar Behnam Monastery may have been established as early as the fourth century CE. It was a Syriac Catholic monastery located in the village Khidr Ily in northern Iraq.

name: Mar Elian
description: The beginnings of the early Christian monastery of Saint Elian at Al-Qaryatayn, Syria, are linked with the saint Julian of Emesa. The Medieval iteration of the monastery was badly damaged - or perhaps nearly destroyed - by Islamic State terrorists in 2015.

name: Mar Matta
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 F4 Mar Matta

name: Mar Melki
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D3 Mar Melki

name: Mar Samuel, Simeon
description: The oldest surviving Syriac Orthodox monastery in the world founded in A.D. 397 by Mor Shmu'el (Samuel) and his student Mor Shem'un (Simon) on the ruins of an ancient Assyrian temple.

name: Mar Yaqub
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 H2 Mar Yaqub

name: Mar Yohannon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 D3 Mar Yohannon

name: Marathos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 A4 Marathos

name: Marchito
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 E4 Marchito

name: Marcianum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 F4 Marcianum

name: Marcianus Fundus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 G4 Marcianus Fundus

name: Margerides
description: Remains of a large religious complex, consisting of a fanum, two small subsidiary structures, and three altars.

name: Marialba Paleochristian Basilica
description: Marialba Paleochristian Basilica dates to the fourth and fifth centuries.

name: Marica
description: Marica, an Italic goddess, was worshipped in a sacred grove located between Minturnae and the estuary of the Liris river.

name: Marimbault Roman Villa
description: The 12th-century Eglise Saint-Vincent de Marimbault was built atop a probable Gallo-Roman villa.

name: Marino Mithraeum
description: A Mithraic shrine of unusual size discovered at modern Marino, Italy in 1963.

name: Marisa
description: A Judean, Edomite, and later Hellenistic settlement. Destroyed ca. 40 BCE by the Parthians.

name: Markesi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 G3 Markesi

name: Marmara sanctuary
description: An ancient sanctuary, perhaps sacred to Demeter, that was discovered during tunneling for the Corinth to Patras highway. The terraced site is located at Marmara, SSE of Aigeira. It dates from the seventh century BCE to the third century BCE.

name: Marmoutier Abbey
description: The Marmoutier Abbey located near Tours, Indre-et-Loire, France is also known as the Abbey of Marmoutier or Marmoutiers. Saint Martin of Tours (316-397) founded the abbey in 372 after he became Bishop of Tours in 371.

name: Mars, Aedes
description: A temple sacred to Mars located on the Via Appia. The shrine was located between the first and second milestones and was dedicated in the fourth century BC. According to Livy, Titus Quinctius dedicated the temple on 1 June 387 BC.

name: Martyrion at Aphrodisias
description: The Roman Tetrakionion at Aphrodisias has been identified as a Martyrion. Its date of construction is uncertain, ranging from as early as the late second century CE to as late as the 5th-6th century CE.

name: Martyrios
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 70 G2 Martyrios

name: Masamas
description: The Gallo-Roman sanctuary with twin temples was discovered in 1964 at the locality called Masamas in the commune of Saint-Léomer.

name: Mashnaqa
description: Mashnaqa, Lebanon, is the site of a monumental altar that dates to the first and second centuries A.D.

name: Matzanni
description: A sanctuary that includes a Punic temple built in white limestone, a nuraghe, and a sacred well.

name: Mausoleum and Sacred area of Hecatomnus
description: A mausoleum and sacred precinct associated with Hecatomnus of Mylasa, who was the satrap of Caria under Artaxerxes II (404–358 BC).

name: Mausoleum of Diocletian
description: A mausoleum built to entomb Diocletian at his retirement palace that was constructed around 295 CE and is located in modern day Split, Croatia.

name: Mausoleum of Galla Placidia
description: An ancient funerary chapel, part of the Santa Croce complex in Ravenna, Italy, possibly built as an imperial mausoleum (ca. AD 417 - 425). The empress Galla Placidia (d. AD 450 ) is the presumed, likely patron of the building, though she is very unlikely to have been entombed within one of three sarcophagi found inside.

name: Mausoleum of Theodoric
description: A monument built in Ravenna, Italy, ca. AD 520 by Theodoric the Great as his future tomb. After Belisarius reconquered Ravenna in 540, the tomb was converted into the church of Sta. Maria della Rotonda.

name: Mavrovouni
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 E4 Mavrovouni

name: Mavrovounoi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Mavrovounoi

name: Mdoukha
description: Roman sanctuary with two small temples.

name: Mechel
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 D3 Mechel

name: Medinet Habu
description: The New Kingdom mortuary temple of Ramesses III at Medinet Habu, on the West bank of the Nile near Luxor.

name: Mefitis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B3 Mefitis

name: Megaloi Theoi (sanctuary, Imbros)
description: A sanctuary of the "Great Gods" on the Aegean island of Imbros/Gökçeada. The remains of the sanctuary are located southwest of modern Kaleköy, to the west of the airport in the vicinity of a ruined monastery of Agios Konstantinos at a place formerly known as Roxado.

name: Megaloi Theoi (sanctuary, Samothrace)
description: The "Sanctuary of the great gods" on the island of Samothrace, one of the chief Panhellenic sanctuaries of the ancient Greek world.

name: Megiste Laura Saba
description: An Eastern Orthodox Christian monastery overlooking the Kidron Valley located between the Old City of Jerusalem and the Dead Sea.

name: Melpeia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 B3 Melpeia

name: Men Askaenos, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 F5 Men Askaenos, T.

name: Men Karou, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 A2 Men Karou, T.

name: Menelaion
description: An ancient Greek sanctuary of Helen and Menelaos near ancient Therapnai in Greece.

name: Menestheus, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 26 D5 Menestheus, T.

name: Mercurius, Aedes
description: A temple dedicated to Mercury and Maia that was located on the slopes of the Aventine Hill overlooking the Circus Maximus.

name: Merjiyat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 B2 Merjiyat

name: Meroe
description: Meroë is an ancient city located on the east bank of the Nile river near Shendi, Sudan. The city was a center of the kingdom of Kush.

name: Meroth
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C3 Meroth

name: Mes Aynak
description: The site of Mes Aynak in modern Afghanistan flourished between the between the fifth and seventh centuries A.D. More than 400 Buddha statues, stupas, and a 40 ha monastery complex have been documented at the site.

name: Messon
description: An extensive ancient sanctuary complex on the island of Lesbos, thought to have been dedicated to Zeus, Hera and Dionysos and dating back to at least the 7th century BCE. A Christian basilica was built on the site in the late fourth century CE.

name: Metanoia Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 53 B2 Metanoia Mon.

name: Metapa
description: According to Polybius, this ancient settlement of Aetolia was razed by Philip of Macedon. Stephanus of Byzantium places Metapa in Acarnania. Pritchett (SAGT) meticulously describes the vicinity and identifies the site of Metapa based on ancient stone blocks in conditions of reuse and abandonment, as well as plentiful ceramic sherds in the vicinity of an older and modern church of the Analipsis on the lake shore just east of the modern village of Daphnias.

name: Meteora
description: Metéora comprises a complex of six Eastern Orthodox monasteries built on natural sandstone pillars of the Thessalian plain. The site was listed as a UNESCO World Heritage site in 1988.

name: Meter apo Speleou, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 E2 Meter apo Speleou, T.

name: Meter Phileis, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 A5 Meter Phileis, T.

name: Meter Steunene, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 C3 Meter Steunene, T.

name: Meter Theon 1, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 D3 Meter Theon 1, T.

name: Meter Theon 2, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 D3 Meter Theon 2, T.

name: Meter Theon, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A5 Meter Theon, T.

name: Meter Theon, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 E4 Meter Theon, T.

name: Metokhi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C5 Metokhi

name: Metroon at Olympia
description: Temple at Olympia constructed in the late 4th or early 3rd century BCE

name: Metropolis (sanctuary)
description: A classical sanctuary, located somewhere near Olpai.

name: Metsopay-Vank
description: Armenian monastery and find place for a Urartian stele.

name: Mezgura
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 D3 Mezgura

name: Mézin
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F1 Mézin

name: Middle Mithraeum
description: Second phase of a sacred space dedicated to the worship of the god Mithras at Dura-Europos.

name: Military Zeus Temple
description: Small temple with pitched roof presumably dedicated to Zeus, located in the military campus zone Block A1 at Dura-Europos.

name: Minatiacum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 E3 Minatiacum

name: Minerva Cabardiacensis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 F4 Minerva Cabardiacensis

name: Minerva temple at Punta della Campanella
description: An ancient temple of Minerva, located on the ancient cape that also was known by the goddess' name.

name: Minor Acropolis, Norba
description: The so-called Minor Acropolis is located on the hill to the south of the Porta Maggiore. This area is surrounded by the urban enclosure wall and is terraced in polygonal masonry. Two platform temples of unknown assignation are to be found here.

name: Mithraeum at Casa del Mitreo in Emerita Augusta
description: The well-preserved remains of a second century Mithraeum at Emerita Augusta (mod. Merida, Spain).

name: Mithraeum at Fertőrákos
description: A well-preserved Mithraeum with modern stone vault above ground and site below in Fertőrákos, Hungary, at the site of ancient Scarbantia.

name: Mithraeum at Mundelsheim
description: A second-century Mithraeum is located in the area of the municipality of Mundelsheim in the Ludwigsburg district of Baden-Württemberg, Germany.

name: Mithraeum at Riegel
description: A temple once sacred to Mithras located in Riegel am Kaiserstuhl (Baden-Wuerttemberg, Germany).

name: Mithraeum at Segontium
description: A mithraeum was excavated at Segontium (modern Caernarfon) in 1959, ca. 137m from the east corner of the Roman auxiliary fort. The site was abandoned in the late fourth century A.D.

name: Mithraeum at Vindovala
description: A temple sacred to Mithras located at the Roman fort of Vindovala (Rudchester) on Hadrian's Wall.

name: Mithraeum at Vulci
description: A mithraeum was discovered at Vulci in 1975 during excavations of a Roman aristocratic house. The shrine was well preserved with two tauroctony scenes dating to the third century. The site was destroyed in the fourth century A.D.

name: Mithraeum III at Poetovio
description: The third of five shrines sacred to Mithras found near the site of Poetovio.

name: Mithraeum of Felicissimus
description: The so-called Mithraeum of Felicissimus is a Mithraic shrine located in ancient Ostia. The shrine itself was installed in a second-century CE building and likely dates to the later third-century CE.

name: Mithraeum of Lucretius Menander
description: The Mithraeum of Lucretius Menander located in ancient Ostia dates to the third century CE.

name: Mithraeum of the Seven Gates
description: The so-called Mithraeum of the Seven Gates was built at Ostia in the second century CE, repurposing rooms that originally date to the previous century.

name: Mithraeum of the Seven Spheres
description: The so-called Mithraeum of the Seven Spheres is located in ancient Ostia near to the theater-cum-quadriporticus complex. The so-called Quattro tempietti screen it from the Decumanus Maximus.

name: Mnajdra Megalithic Temple (Malta)
description: Megalithic temple complex of the fourth millennium BC.

name: Mogao Caves
description: Buddhist cave monastery complex in the cliffs above the Dachuan River near the Dunhaung Oasis, constructed in 366 CE and in use until the 14th Century CE. The caves contain thousands of murals and painted sculptures, and thousands of manuscripts in Chinese, Tibetan, Uyghur, Sogdian, Sanskrit, and Khotanese were stored in the so-called "Library Cave." The Mogao Caves were added to UNESCO's World Heritage List in 1987.

name: Möhn
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 G3 Möhn

name: Mollarella
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 D4 Mollarella

name: Mollis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 B2 Mollis

name: Molzbichl
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 F3 Molzbichl

name: Monasteraki
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C3 Monasteraki

name: Monastero di San Mamiliano
description: The monastery of San Mamiliano located on the island of Montecristo.

name: Monastery of S. Elia Vecchio
description: A ruined ninth century Christian monastery in Calabria.

name: Monastery of Saint John the Theologian
description: The Monastery of Saint John the Theologian (also known as the Monastery of Saint John the Divine) is a Greek Orthodox monastery dating to AD 1088 on the island of Patmos.

name: Monastery of Saint Simeon Stylites
description: The monastery of Saint Simeon Stylites, located ca. 30 km northwest of Aleppo, Syria, is one of the oldest surviving Byzantine churches and dates to the fifth century. The church is built on the site of the pillar of St. Simeon Stylites.

name: Monastery of San Juan de Caaveiro
description: A Christian monastery established in AD 934.

name: Monastery of Simeon Stylites the Younger
description: A sixth-century AD monastery named for Simeon Stylites the Younger who lived atop a pillar on Thaumaston Oros.

name: Monopteros
description: A small round shrine built in the Athenian Agora in the mid-2nd century AD

name: Mons Seleucus
description: Mons Seleucus was the site of the famous battle between Constantius II and the usurper Magnentius in AD 353. The Gallo-Roman vicus at La Bâtie-Montsaléon included a Gallo-Roman sanctuary.

name: Mont-Rivel
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 18 C3 Mont-Rivel

name: Montbouy
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 H1 Montbouy

name: Monte d'Accoddi
description: A ramped, megalithic structure in Sardinia dated to around 2700-2000 BC.

name: Monte del Fico
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E2 Monte del Fico

name: Monte Li Santi
description: A Faliscan sanctuary located on the slopes of Monte Li Santi.

name: Monte Monaco di Gioia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 F3 Monte Monaco di Gioia

name: Monte Papalucio
description: The sanctuary of Monte Papalucio was an important Messapian sanctuary. It is located on the mountain of the same name in the territory of Oria in the province of Brindisi. The deities Demeter and Persephone were venerated in this cave sanctuary.

name: Monte Poliscia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 D4 Monte Poliscia

name: Monte S. Martino
description: The remains of a multi-period pre-Roman and Roman sanctuary.

name: Monte Torre Maggiore
description: An Italic temple complex in Umbria of the first millennium B.C. that includes the so-called "Ara maior".

name: Monte Tosto
description: An Etruscan and Roman site lying 4 km to the west of Caere along the monumental road leading to Pyrgi. Until the early 1960s, the site was known for the monumental tumulus that Mengarelli had excavated in the 1920s. Subsequent excavation revealed other evidence, including an elite compound that served as a sanctuary.

name: Montebuono
description: The remains of a villa were excavated near the church of San Pietro ad centum muros in the nineteenth century. Based on epigraphic evidence, the site is connected with M. Vipsanius Agrippa.

name: Monteverde
description: The eleventh century monastery of Santa Maria di Monteverde sits atop and reuses elements of Roman and pre-Roman settlements on the hilltop.

name: Montreuil-Bellay
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 E1 Montreuil-Bellay

name: Mortuary Temple of Amenhotep III
description: Temple constructed in the Theban Necropolis during the 18th Dynasty.

name: Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut
description: The Mortuary Temple of Hatshepsut at Deir el-Bahari was built for the Eighteenth dynasty Pharaoh Hatshepsut.

name: Mortuary temple of Mentuhotep II
description: The Mortuary temple of the Eleventh Dynasty pharaoh Mentuhotep II at Deir el-Bahari would inspire the later mortuary complexes of Hatshepsut and Thutmose III that are also found at the site.

name: Mortuary Temple of Merenptah
description: Mortuary temple of the Nineteenth Dynasty in the Theban Necropolis.

name: Mortuary Temple of Senwosret III
description: Mortuary temple constructed for Senwosret III, a pharaoh of the 12th Dynasty in Egypt.

name: Morulls
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 F4 Morulls

name: Msellata
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 F2 Msellata

name: Mselliten
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 G3 Mselliten

name: Muk
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C4 Muk

name: Mura Abbandonate, Sanctuary of Fondo Decina
description: A terraced sanctuary of the Samnite culture that was in use from the archaic period to the third century B.C.

name: Musaifna
description: Excavations at Tell Museifneh (Eski Mosul) in the 1980s revealed remains of a well-built stone building that was identified as a Christian basilica dating to the seventh century A.D. The apisdal basilica measured ca. 50 x 50 meters.

name: Musawwarat
description: A Meroitic temple complex in modern Sudan, located 180 km northeast of Khartoum, 20 km north of Naqa and approximately 25 km south-east of the Nile. The complex dates to the early Meroitic period (third century B.C.). In 2011 the site was enrolled as a UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Mushabbak Basilica
description: The remains of a well-preserved Byzantine church (ca. 470) located five km west of the Monastery of St Simeon. The ruins lie near the town of Daret A'zzeh.

name: Mut el-Kharab
description: The settlement of Mut el-Kharab, located in Egypt's Dakhleh Oasis. A temple to the god Seth was built at this location.

name: Mygdaleza basilica
description: The remains of an Early Christian basilica dating to the fifth and sixth centuries.

name: Myrtou
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Myrtou

name: Mysia (settlement)
description: An ancient settlement in the Argolid, mentioned by Pausanias, who highlights there a sanctuary of Demeter. This settlement is probably to be identified with the modern Phyktia.

name: Nabû temple (Khorsabad)
description: The eighth-century-BC Assyrian king Sargon II (r. 721–705 BC) had a temple to the god Nabû, the god of scribes, built in the citadel of his new capital, Dūr-Šarrukīn (modern Khorsabad). Building H is the largest temple in Sargon’s newly-constructed city and it was built on a platform in the western side of the citadel, rather than on the palace terrace. The Nabû temple was connected to the temple district, which formed part of Sargon’s royal residence, by a stone bridge.

name: Nagos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 C4 Nagos

name: Naisar
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 G3 Naisar

name: Nālandā Mahavihara
description: Buddhist monastery and educational center founded during the Gupta Empire (5th century CE). The site was destroyed around 1200 CE. It was located in ancient and medieval Magadha (modern-day Bihar), eastern India.

name: Nanteuil-sur-Aisne
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 E3 Nanteuil-sur-Aisne

name: Naqsh-i Bahram
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 B3 Naqsh-i Bahram

name: Naqsh-i Rajab
description: An archaeological site with rock-cut reliefs made under the Sasanian kings Ardašir I (224-241) and Shapur I (241-272).

name: Narbek
description: A rock-cut church located about 5km west of the modern town of Omar Mukhtar in Libya.

name: Narmouthis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 75 D2 Narmouthis

name: Natanz
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 G3 Natanz

name: Naudaran
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 C5 Naudaran

name: Navan
description: A ceremonial site of pre-Christian Gaelic Ireland and the capital of the Ulaid. The site, located near Armagh, Northern Ireland, consists of a fortified settlement with a ceremonial building.

name: Naveta of Torre Llisà
description: A naviform temple located on Minorca.

name: Navish
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 F2 Navish

name: Nea Moni
description: An eleventh-century monastery on Chios built by Constantine IX Monomachos. The site was listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1990.

name: Nebi Safa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C2 Nebi Safa

name: Nebo Mons (church)
description: Site of a modern church and monastery atop Mount Nebo in modern Jordan on a site commemorated by earlier churches built on a site traditionally held to be the burial location of the prophet Moses. The first ancient church here is mentioned earliest in the itinerary of Egeria.

name: Neklone
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 75 D2 Neklone

name: Nekromanteion
description: An ancient sanctuary of necromancy sacred to Hades and Persephone located on the banks of the Acheron River in Epirus, near the ancient city of Ephyra.

name: Nemea
description: Nemea, located north of Argos, was the site of the Panhellenic Games and the Sanctuary of Nemean Zeus.

name: Nemea Early Christian basilica
description: An Early Christian basilica located southeast of ancient Nemea.

name: Nemora Vacunae
description: Shrine dedicated to a rural divinity of the Sabina.

name: Nemrut Dağ
description: A major sanctuary of Coammagene and site of the mausoleum of Antiochus I (69–34 B.C.). A UNESCO World Heritage Site located on modern Mount Nemrut in Turkey's Adıyaman province.

name: Nemus Sorabense/Sorabile
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 48 B2 Nemus Sorabense/Sorabile

name: Nessana
description: Nessana is an ancient site located in the Negev Desert. Established during the Hellenistic period, Nessana has numerous occupational phases including as the site of a Roman military garrison in the fourth century CE. The site flourishes in connection with the Christian pilgrimage route linking the Holy Land to Mount Sinai (sixth and seventh centuries CE).

name: Nettleton
description: A significant cult center at Nettleton Shrub in Wiltshire was dedicated to Apollo Cunomaglus.

name: Neuvy-sur-Barangeon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 H1 Neuvy-sur-Barangeon

name: New Basilica of Saint Apollinaris
description: A basilica in Ravenna, Italy, erected by Theodoric the Great in the first quarter of the sixth century AD.

name: New Year's Festival House at Assur
description: As part of his post-689-BC religious reforms, the Neo-Assyrian king Sennacherib (704–681 BC) had a new New Year’s festival house (akītu-house) constructed outside the western wall of Assur. This was an attempt to replicate the cultic topography of Babylon. The Sumerian ceremonial names of that holy building and its cella are Eabbaugga ("House Where Tiāmat Is Put To Death") and Edubdubabba ("House That Makes Tiāmat Tremble”) respectively.

name: Neyrac-les-Bains
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 C4 Neyrac-les-Bains

name: Nihata
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C2 Nihata

name: Nike (Monument at Marathon)
description: A monument to the Battle of Marathon. Eugene Vanderpool describes the location of the monument as lying in the northeastern part of the plain of Marathon. A Christian chapel known as the Panagia Mesosporitissa marks the vicinity of this monument. In the area of the chapel are a well that reuses ancient marble and the ruins of a medieval tower that was built from re-used ancient blocks, including a massive Ionic column capital and two large column drums.

name: Ningal temple (Khorsabad)
description: As part of the palatial complex of Dūr-Šarrukīn (modern Khorsabad), the eighth-century-BC Assyrian king Sargon II (r. 721–705 BC) constructed six temples, including one to the goddess Ningal.

name: Ninurta temple (Kalhu)
description: When Kalhu (biblical Calah, modern Nimrud) became the principal administrative center of the Assyrian Empire in the ninth century BC, king Ashurnasirpal II (r. 883–859 BC) built (or rebuilt) numerous temples. The most important of those was dedicated to the god Ninurta, the patron deity of city, which Ashurnasirpal had constructed between his palace and the ziggurat, a building also consecrated to Ninurta.

name: Ninurta temple (Khorsabad)
description: As part of the palatial complex of Dūr-Šarrukīn (modern Khorsabad), the eighth-century-BC Assyrian king Sargon II (r. 721–705 BC) constructed six temples, including one to the god Ninurta.

name: Nistos
description: A sanctuary on Mont Sacon at an elevation of 1541 meters above sea level.

name: Northern Cluster
description: The Northern Cluster of rock-cut churches at Lalibela.

name: Northern sanctuary at Kythnos
description: Religious structure in Kythnos constructed in the Archaic period.

name: Nostra Segnora de Mesumundu
description: Nostra Segnora de Mesumundu ("Our Lady of Mesumundu") is located in the territory of Siligo, Sardinia, Italy. This archaeological site preserves remains of multiple architectural phases. A mid-Imperial (likely second-century CE) Roman complex, which included baths, was reused, in part, for the construction of a Christian church in the sixth century.

name: Notre-Dame-de-Laval
description: A pre-Roman and Gallo-Roman sanctuary at the locality called l'Ermitage de Notre-Dame-de-Laval.

name: Nubian Lion Temple at Naqa
description: The Temple of Apedemak, also known as the Lion Temple, is located to the west of the temple of Amun.

name: Nymphaeum of Egeria
description: A nymphaeum connected with Egeria, the divine advisor of Numa Pompilius. This iteration of Egeria's spring dates to the time of Herodes Atticus in the second century A.D.

name: Nymphaion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C2 Nymphaion

name: Nymphaion/Balaneion Tiberinon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 B4 Nymphaion/Balaneion Tiberinon

name: Oberdorla
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 12 D1 Oberdorla

name: Oberlienz
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E3 Oberlienz

name: Octon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 A2 Octon

name: Odry
description: The modern Polish city of Odry is the site of a major "stone circles" cemetery of the iron age Wielbark Culture.

name: Oisseau-Le-Petit
description: Oisseau-Le-Petit is the site of a Gallo-Roman fanum.

name: Old Bouleuterion/Metroon
description: The Old Bouleuterion on the west side of the Agora in Athens was built in the early fifth century BC as the meeting place of the Boule or Council of 500. It also housed the Metroon, a shrine to the Mother of the Gods. The Council moved to the New Bouleuterion at the end of the fifth century BC, but the Old Bouleuterion remained in use as an archive and shrine. It was renovated ca. 140 BC and destroyed in the Herulian Sack of 267 AD. In the early 5th century AD, one of the rooms was rebuilt, possibly for use as a synagogue.

name: Older Parthenon
description: A large temple on the Acropolis of Athens, begun in the 480s BC and destroyed during the Persian Sack of 480 BC, while still under construction. Parts of the structure were incorporated into the north wall of the Acropolis and other structures in the mid-fifth century BC and the Parthenon was built on the same site, using some of the older structure's foundations, beginning in 447 BC.

name: Olympia
description: Ancient sanctuary of Zeus and home of the ancient Olympic games.

name: Olympieion
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 G4 Olympieion

name: Omboi
description: The Ptolemaic temple to Sobek and Horus the Elder at Kom Ombo.

name: Onia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 G1 Onia

name: Optatianai, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 52 F3 Optatianai, Mon.

name: Oracular sanctuary at Oinoe
description: Located to the east of the medieval tower at Oinoe are the remains of a rectangular peristylar building measuring approximately 13.60 x 17.30 meters. The peristyle used marble columns set atop marble pilasters. Spyridon Marinatos discovered the site in 1972.

name: Orbaniacum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 G1 Orbaniacum

name: Örendibi
description: Modern name for ruins of a Byzantine church and surrounding rural settlement in eastern Rough Cilicia

name: Orsingen
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 A2 Orsingen

name: Oscata di Sopra
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B2 Oscata di Sopra

name: Osireion
description: The Osireion at Abydos is located to the rear of the temple of Seti I. It is an integral part of Seti I's funeral complex and was built to resemble an 18th Dynasty Valley of the Kings tomb.

name: Ostia Synagogue
description: The ancient synagogue located in Ostia Antica is one of the oldest known synagogues in the world. The structure's earliest phase dates to the reign of Claudius and it continued in use until the fifth century.

name: Oulippada?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 5 inset Oulippada?

name: Pachyammos sanctuary
description: The remains of a robbed-out Hellenistic temple complex were excavated at Pachyammos to the south of Paphos, Cyprus, between 2015 and 2019. The site included a peripteral temple in antis.

name: Pagai
description: A fortified port located on the Gulf of Corinth near Alepokhori.

name: Pagania Sanctuary (Aigies, Laconia)
description: A rural sanctuary, probably of Artemis, located about a kilometer north-northwest of modern Aigies in Laconia. It is surely to be associated with ancient Aigiai, which was located less than two kilometers to the southwest.

name: Pagans Hill Roman Temple
description: A Romano-British temple excavated on Pagans Hill at Chew Stoke, Somerset.

name: Pagus Assalitanus
description: Pagus Assalitanus, located in Africa Proconsularis, lies some 6 km southeast of Thubursicu Bure.

name: Palaio Pretorio
description: The ruins of a colonnaded structure in marble, perhaps a temple; a well / cistern is also observed by Sealy.

name: Palaiomonastero
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 D2 Palaiomonastero

name: Palazzo Barberini Mithraeum
description: The Palazzo Barberini Mithraeum numbers among the best-preserved mithraic shrines in Rome. It is situated in via delle Quattro Fontane. Construction work revealed the site in 1936. A portion of the original structures of the second century CE was transformed into the mithraeum in the third century.

name: Paleochristian basilica
description: A Paleochristian basilica was discovered at the eighth mile of the Via Tiburtina in 2003. The subterranean basilica reused an earlier quarry and may be related to San Sinforosa. The site dates from the late fourth to the middle of the fifth century CE.

name: Paleochristian basilica at Vravrona
description: An early Christian basilica located west of ancient Brauron in Attica.

name: paleochristian basilica of Lugdunum Convenarum
description: The paleochristian basilica of Saint-Bertrand-de-Comminges.

name: Paleochristian basilica, Illa del Rei
description: The Paleochristian basilica located on Illa del Rei dates to the sixth century.

name: Paleochristian Church, Lin
description: A paleochristian church located in Lin, Korçë County, Albania, with fourth- and fifth-century mosaics.

name: Palike
description: An ancient settlement in east central Sicily, atop the Rocchicella, which included a sanctuary to the Divine Palikoi (a pair of boiling lakes) within a grotto found on the hillside.

name: Palladion
description: A sanctuary in Attica, containing the Palladion, an image of Athena captured during the Trojan War by the Argives, who were killed by Damophon and the Athenians on their way home. The sanctuary was the location of a lawcourt for cases of involuntary homicide, attempted homicide, and homicide of a foreigner, resident foreigner or enslaved person. It was probably located in south-eastern Athens or perhaps in Phaleron.

name: Pampligua
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 H2 Pampligua

name: Pan, Cave of
description: The Phyle Cave located on Mount Parnes near Fyli (Phyle), Greece, is a small cave that served in antiquity as a shrine sacred to the god Pan. It is one of five such cave sanctuaries dedicated to Pan known in ancient Attica.

name: Panagia Angeloktistos
description: The church of Panagia tis Angeloktistis or Panagia Angeloktisti is a Byzantine church located in the village of Kiti, Cyprus. Its origins lies in the fifth and sixth centuries. It was listed as a tentative UNESCO World Heritage site in 2015.

name: Panagia Arkoudiotissa
description: The Arkoudiotissa Cave on Crete has been the site of ritual activity since antiquity. The cave's cultic association stems from a rock formation that looks like a bear (Arkoudiotissa ("she-bear")).

name: Panagia Ekatontapiliani
description: Panagia Ekatontapiliani or Panagia Katapoliani is an important Early Christian church complex in Parikia town, on the island of Paros, Greece, that was founded in 326 CE.

name: Panagia Kanakaria
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 E2 Panagia Kanakaria

name: Panagia tis Kyras
description: A twelfth-century CE Greek Orthodox church located north of modern Livadia on Cyprus' Karpasia peninsula. Its apse had been adapted from part of an older building and contained a 7th-century mosaic of the Theotokos (i.e., Mary, mother of Jesus).

name: Pandroseion
description: An open-air precinct dedicated to Pandrosos, located on the Acropolis of Athens, at the west side of the Erechtheion. It contained the sacred olive tree of Athena and an altar of Zeus Herkeios.

name: Panetelle
description: A sanctuary with votive deposits dating to the Hellenistic period at Panetelle di Mondragone.

name: Panionion
description: Located near Mt. Mycale, the Panionion was an Ionian sanctuary dedicated to Poseidon Helikonios that served as the meeting place of the Ionian League.

name: Panóias
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 24 D3 Panóias

name: Pānṛ I
description: Buddhist temple complex in Swat, Pakistan occupied from the 1st century CE until the end of the 4th-5th centuries CE.

name: Pantheon
description: A temple in the Campus Martius of Rome that was first built by Marcus Agrippa (ca. 27-25 B.C.) and then re-built by the emperor Hadrian (after A.D. 126). In A.D. 609 the structure was consecrated as a Christian church, S. Maria ad Martyres.

name: Paphor, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 77 E2 Paphor, Mon.

name: Paptoulis?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 81 C2 Paptoulis?

name: Parco archeologico Griso-Laboccetta
description: An Archaic to Hellenistic sanctuary sacred to Demeter.

name: Parco delle Tombe di via Latina
description: A cluster of Roman Imperial tombs along the Via Latina excavated in the nineteenth century.

name: Parembole?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 81 C2 Parembole?

name: Parthenion, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 23 G4 Parthenion, T.

name: Parthenion? Akron
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 23 G4 Parthenion? Akron

name: Parthenon
description: The temple of Athena Parthenos located on the Acropolis of Athens, which was constructed at the height of Athenian power between 447 and 432 BCE. It is the largest Doric temple in mainland Greece.

name: Passy
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 17 H2 Passy

name: Patriacum
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 H2 Patriacum

name: Patriarchal Basilica of Santa Maria Assunta of Aquileia
description: The Basilica di Santa Maria Assunta, Aquileia dates originally to the fourth century.

name: Patriasdorf
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E3 Patriasdorf

name: Pattadakal
description: A group of temples featuring nine Hindu temples and a Jain sanctuary dating to the seventh and eighth centuries CE and connected with the Chalukya dynasty.

name: Pecë
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 B2 Pecë

name: Pelm
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 G2 Pelm

name: Per-Ptah
description: The site of Per-Ptah, meaning "House of Ptah", lies some 90 km south of Aswan and received a temple dedicated to pharaoh Ramesses II. Some of the architectural and sculptural remains of the temple were dismantled and relocated to the site of New Kalabsha in the 1960s during the construction of the Aswan High Dam.

name: Pera Milous
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 58 G2 Pera Milous

name: Pergamos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Pergamos

name: Peroushtitsa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B6 Peroushtitsa

name: Pescara
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 F4 Pescara

name: Petit-Mars
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 D1 Petit-Mars

name: Petrera
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 D2 Petrera

name: Petrera
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 C3 Petrera

name: Pfaffenhofen
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 D2 Pfaffenhofen

name: Phanai
description: A large ancient sanctuary of Apollo, located on the southwest coast of the island of Chios.

name: Phasiane Theos
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 87 G2 Phasiane Theos

name: Philae
description: An ancient island located just above the First cataract of the Nile River that was flooded by the construction of the Aswan High Dam. The island was home to a multi-period temple complex, which was moved to the nearby island of Agilkia and declared a UNESCO World Heritage Site in the 1970s.

name: Philippeion
description: The Philippeion is a tholos at Olympia constructed to celebrate Philip II's victory at Chaeonea in 338 BCE and completed by Alexander the Great.

name: Phlabianai
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 64 B3 Phlabianai

name: Phokikon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 55 D4 Phokikon

name: Phosphoros/Lux Dubia (sanctuary)
description: Strabo describes this sanctuary in his narrative of sailing up the Guadalquivir River. His text mentions a sanctuary of Phosphorus - this is likely Diana - that is known as "Lux Dubia" (Strabo transliterates "Lucem dubiam" to Greek characters, thus λοῦκεμ δουβίαν).

name: Piana del Lago Sanctuary
description: An Etruscan sanctuary that was founded at the end of the 5th century BCE and saw activity as late as the reign of Nero. Evidence has also been found for activity in the area during the Neolithic and the Bronze Age. The site is located on the southern shore of the Lac de Bolsena in the modern Italian municipality of Montefiascone.

name: Pianabella
description: A large Christian basilica discovered in 1976 several hundred meters southeast of the Porta Laurentina of Ostia. The original phase dates to the end of the fourth century A.D.

name: Piano dei Tivoli
description: Archaeological excavations conducted at the locality known as Piano dei Tivoli revealed, by means of numerous fragments of architectural terracottas, a sanctuary of the Hellenistic period. There is also evidence of settlement during the fifth and fourth centuries BCE.

name: Pietra Rossa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 47 E4 Pietra Rossa

name: Pieve di San Giovanni Battista a Sillano
description: A ruined church that was once part of the diocese of Volterra, San Giovanni Battista a Sillano is unique in Tuscany for having a Romanesque-Norman façade. The ruined edifice likely dates to the twelfth and thirteenth centuries although the parish exists as early as the mid-tenth century.

name: Pieve di San Pietro ad Mensulas
description: A Romanesque church located in the Italian comune of Sinalunga (Siena). Saint Donatus of Arezzo may have founded the church in the fourth century.

name: Pieve Socana
description: Pieve di Sant'Antonino a Socana, a Medieval parish church, is built atop the remains of an Etruscan sanctuary.

name: Pigades
description: A Late Bronze Age settlement and sanctuary.

name: Pigi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 D2 Pigi

name: Pillerhöhe
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 C2 Pillerhöhe

name: Pirdop
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 22 B6 Pirdop

name: Pithiviers-le-Vieil
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 C4 Pithiviers-le-Vieil

name: Pizzica
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 E4 Pizzica

name: Plataea
description: Plataea is an ancient city of Boeotia, located south of Thebes. In 479 BC Plataea was the site of the Battle of Plataea, in which an allied Greek army defeated the Persians. Plataea was destroyed in the Peloponnesian War by Thebes and Sparta in 427 BC and rebuilt in 386 BC.

name: Plöckenpass
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 19 E3 Plöckenpass

name: Ploutonion at Hierapolis
description: The Ploutonion at Hierapolis is a walled sanctuary dedicated to Pluto and Kore.

name: Poggio Colla
description: An Etruscan settlement located in the Mugello valley.

name: Pommern-Martberg
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 H2 Pommern-Martberg

name: Pompeiacum/Vernemetis
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 F4 Pompeiacum/Vernemetis

name: Portara on Naxos
description: The Portara is the 26-foot-tall doorway of an unfinished Ionic temple of the Late Archaic Period on the island of Naxos.

name: Porto Cesareo
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 G4 Porto Cesareo

name: Portonaccio sanctuary at Veii
description: The extraurban sanctuary of Minerva at Portonaccio is an important Etruscan cult center. The temple dates ca. 510 B.C. and was adorned with acroterial sculptures, notably the famous "Apollo of Veii".

name: Poseidon Phytalmios?, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A4 Poseidon Phytalmios?, T.

name: Poseidon Porthmios, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 60 E4 Poseidon Porthmios, T.

name: Poseidon, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 86 C2 Poseidon, T.

name: Poseidon?, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 61 A3 Poseidon?, T.

name: Poseidon?, T.
description: Poseidon?, T. a sanctuary of Poseidon on the island of Samos.

name: Potamos, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 62 F2 Potamos, T.

name: Pouzauges
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 E2 Pouzauges

name: Pozzo Pantaleo
description: The 'Pozzo Pantaleo' is a Roman mausoleum that made use of a cistern of the early Imperial period. In a third phase the site was repurposed as a shrine dedicated to the Christian Saint Pantaleon.

name: Praeneste
description: An ancient city of central Italy, the eighth and seventh century B.C. origins of which demonstrate high-level trade contact with the Eastern Mediterranean world. The city withdrew from the Latin League (499 B.C.) and opposed Rome in the Latin war; Cincinnatus eventually subdued Praeneste. Sulla founded a new colony there in the first century B.C.

name: Pranu Muttedu
description: Pranu Muttedu is a pre- and protohistoric archaeological site located near Goni, Sardinia. The complex includes standing stones (menhirs) and prehistoric built tombs (domus de janas). The site dates to the Late Neolithic (3200 – 2800 BC) and Early Chalcolithic (2800 – 2600 BC) periods.

name: Prdejci
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 50 B2 Prdejci

name: Pre-Erechtheion
description: A small temple on the Acropolis of Athens that might have existed from the 470s B.C. until replaced by the Erechtheion, perhaps in the 420s B.C. It might have hosted the cult of Athena Polias and/or Erechtheus.

name: Preah Khan
description: Temple at Angkor dedicated in 1191 CE under Jayavarman VII. The temple entered a period of decline beginning in the 15th century.

name: Preah Ko
description: Temple constructed in the city of Hariharalaya, dedicated in 880 CE by the Khmer king Indravarman I.

name: Preclassical naiskos in the Parthenon
description: A small shrine with an altar built before 480 BC on the Acropolis of Athens. The northern colonnade of the Parthenon was built around this shrine, preserving it in situ. It is unknown what cult it housed.

name: Prepelaion?
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 56 E5 Prepelaion?

name: Prinias (Patela)
description: An Iron Age settlement on the Patela plateau north of the modern village of Prinias; its ancient name is uncertain. The site is notable for its occupation from the end of the Bronze Age through to the Archaic period, as well as for the monumental architecture and Orientalizing sculpture of its Buildings ('Temples') A and B.

name: Profitis Elias
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 C4 Profitis Elias

name: Prophetes Elias Troullakiou
description: An ancient settlement on the island of Siphnos. The current church sits on Byzantine foundations and is surrounded by remains of older fortifications and other structures.

name: Prummu?/Primii?
description: The one-time capital of the ancient Kerma culture in Nubia.

name: Psaropyrgos
description: An ancient temple of Poseidon, known today as the "Tomb of Homer", located on a height at the northern end of the Aegean island of Ios. Some funerary epigraphy has also been noted at the site.

name: Ptghnavank
description: Church building in the modern village of Ptghni, Kotayk province, Armenia, dated to the 6th-7th centuries CE.

name: Ptoou Nkalamon
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 75 D3 Ptoou Nkalamon

name: Punic-Roman Sanctuary, Kamart
description: A Punic-Roman sanctuary, possibly of Hygieia/Salus or Aesculapius/Eshmun was identified to the northwest of Carthage in the direction of Cape Kamart (Tunisia), mostly on the basis of an assemblage of extremely fragmentary polychromatic terracotta statues, numbering at least 30.

name: Punta della Vipera Etruscan temple
description: A coastal Etruscan sanctuary that dates ca. 540-530 B.C. with additional architectural phases dating to the fourth through second centuries B.C.

name: Pusht-i Arisht
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 92 B3 Pusht-i Arisht

name: Puy-Lautard
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 G3 Puy-Lautard

name: Pyra
description: Pyra is a place with preserved ruins of a Doric temple dating to the third century B.C. The sources connect Pyra with the pyre of the hero Herakles.

name: Pyramid-complex of Senusret III
description: Funerary complex of King Senusret III at Dahshur including a pyramid.

name: Pyrgi
description: Pyrgi is an ancient Etruscan port city on the Tyrrhenian coast of Italy. A Roman colony was established at the site by 191 B.C. In 1964, the bilingual 'Pyrgi Plaques' were discovered during the course of excavations.

name: Pythion
description: The sanctuary of Apollo Pythios in Athens, located south of the Temple of Olympian Zeus, probably near Ioseph ton Rogon street, where many inscriptions related to the cult have been found.

name: Qadamgah
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 D4 Qadamgah

name: Qadboun
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 B3 Qadboun

name: Qafr Zebad
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 C2 Qafr Zebad

name: Qal’at Sem’an/Church of Saint Simeon Stylites
description: An ancient church, located in modern Syria. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."

name: Qalaa
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 B5 Qalaa

name: Qalaat Faqra
description: A Roman-era sanctuary complex in Lebanon with multiple altars and two temples. On the basis of epigraphic evidence, one of these temples seems to have been dedicated to Atargatis and the other possibly to Zeus Beelgalassos. There is some evidence for worship also of Zeus Heliopolis and for the Roman imperial cult. A Byzantine basilica was later built on the site.

name: Qalat el-Baben
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B4 Qalat el-Baben

name: Qalb Lozé
description: An ancient place, located in modern Syria, with remains of an ancient basilica. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."

name: Qalbloze
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 D4 Qalbloze

name: Qanat-i Bagh
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 94 B4 Qanat-i Bagh

name: Qasr el-Aguz
description: Site in Luxor (Egypt) associated with a Ptolemaic temple dedicated to the god Thoth.

name: Qasr el-Banat
description: The remains of an ancient temple at Qasr el Banat, Lebanon,

name: Qasr el-Sagha temple
description: Temple located north of Lake Qarun in the Fayyum Depression. Tentatively dated to the Old Kingdom period, the temple may have been dedicated to the god Sobek.

name: Qasr Libya, West Church
description: At Qasr Libya, the ancient Theodorias, two churhes have been identified. The Byzantine settlement was refounded under Justinian in 539 CE.

name: Qasr Naous
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 68 A5 Qasr Naous

name: Qasr Neba
description: Qasr Neba is the site of a Roman temple in the Bekaa valley of Lebanon.

name: Qasr Nemrud
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 69 D2 Qasr Nemrud

name: Qasr Serij
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 89 E4 Qasr Serij

name: Qenneshre
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 67 G3 Qenneshre

name: Quarrush
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 37 B1 Quarrush

name: Quattro tempietti
description: The sanctuary along the Decumanus Maximus of Ostia known as the "Quattro tempietti" dates from the Republican period. Based on multiple phases of restoration, they continued in use until at least the second century CE. Epigraphic evidence leads some scholars to conclude that the four temples were sacred to Venus, Fortuna, Ceres, and Spes and had been dedicated by one Publius Lucilius Gamala.

name: Raiano
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 F4 Raiano

name: Ramappa Temple
description: A thirteenth-century temple that was built during the period of the Kakatiya ruler Ganapati Deva.

name: Ramesseum
description: The funerary temple of Ramesses II, built within the area known as the Ipet-isut, "The Most Selected of Places", along the Nile River.

name: Rantidi
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 72 B3 Rantidi

name: Ras Abu el Daraq
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 B3 Ras Abu el Daraq

name: Ras el-Haddagia
description: A sanctuary of Ammon was located at Ras el-Haddagia. The rectangular temple (7.05 x 2.95 m) included an altar and a portico.

name: Ras il-Wardija
description: A Punic-Roman sanctuary site located on the western coast of Gozo (Gaulos), Malta.

name: Re-Buried Hellenistic Temple and Workshops
description: Excavations on site of post office at 49 Ben Ami St. revealed Hellenistic remains, including a dedication to Zeus Soter on behalf of Antiochos VII by a courtier (SEG XIX 9094). This inscription was associated with Hellenistic architecture of a temple-like character. Nearby workshops also uncovered.

name: Reccopolis
description: Reccopolis is an urban, Visigothic, ex-novo foundation made under king Liuvigild in A.D. 578. The archaeological remains at the site include a palace complex, public area, basilica, walls, and aqueduct. The site is known today by the Spanish version of its Latin name (Recópolis); it is located near the modern village of Zorita de los Canes in the Spanish province of Guadelajara.

name: Red Basilica
description: A Roman temple built above the Selinus river was originally constructed to worship Egyptian gods, most likely Isis and/or Serapis. In the Byzantine era, it was converted into a church of St. John. It was subsequently ruined, although the rotunda on the north side is still an active mosque.

name: Red Hill Roman Temple
description: Site of a Roman temple and possible small town near the confluence of the rivers Soar and Trent near Ratclifffe-on-Soar, Nottinghamshire. Findspot of at least three curse tablets (defixiones).

name: Rephidim
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 D4 Rephidim

name: Resras
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 80 B4 Resras

name: Ribemont-sur-Ancre
description: Ribemont-sur-Ancre is the site of a Celtic and Gallo-Roman sanctuary located on the right bank of the Ancre river, a tributary of the Somme. Roger Agache discovered the site in 1962. The site is vast in its area, covering some 80 ha.

name: Ried
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 12 H4 Ried

name: Rijelat Umm Kubar
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 91 C4 Rijelat Umm Kubar

name: Rijsbergen
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 E1 Rijsbergen

name: Risqah
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 76 G3 Risqah

name: Riva S. Vitale
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 D2 Riva S. Vitale

name: Robigo?, T.
description: The sanctuary of the god Robigus, located at the sixth mile of the Via Cassia. The Romans celebrated the festival of Robigalia on 25 April.

name: Roccadaspide
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 B4 Roccadaspide

name: Roccaspromonte
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 G2 Roccaspromonte

name: Rock-cut Sanctuary of Letoids and Nymphs
description: The Elmalı Museum's 2011 discovery of a rock-cut relief and many broken stelai near the fountain known as Sarı Mehmet Çeşmesi, in the Yukarıovacık Yayla (plateau), on the western slopes of Temrencik Tepe, on the border between the territory of ancient Choma and Oinoanda, points to the existence of a spring sanctuary of Leto, her children Apollo and Artemis (the Letoids), and perhaps also 9 nymphs.

name: Roda
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 54 A2 Roda

name: Rogeban
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 35 E3 Rogeban

name: Roma et Augustus, T.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 65 D2 Roma et Augustus, T.

name: Roman spring sanctuary near Heckenmünster
description: An extensive complex of structures dating to the 1st - 3rd centuries of the Roman imperial period sited at a pair of springs known today as "Viktoriaquelle" and "Schwefelquelle," near modern Heckenmünster in German. This site, whose ancient name and tutelary deities are not known, was extensively excavated in the 1960s. It comprised religious and performative spaces, as well as baths and hostelry.

name: Roman Temple at Maarab
description: Site of a Roman temple overlooking Jounieh Bay

name: Roman temple of Córdoba
description: A pseudodipteral, hexastyle, Corinthian order temple begun under Claudius and completed under Domitian. The temple is likely sacred to the imperial cult.

name: Roman Temple of Évora
description: The Roman Temple of Évora, also referred to as the Temple of Diana, is a Roman temple of the first century A.D.

name: Roman temple of Vic
description: The Roman temple of Vic dates to the second century A.D.

name: Roman temple, Capo Mulini
description: The remains of an early Imperial Roman temple on Sicily's Cape Mulini.

name: Roman temples at Chieti
description: Structures thought to be temples set within a sacred area that includes a sacred well.

name: Roquepertuse
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 E2 Roquepertuse

name: Rossano
description: Rossano di Vaglio, a fourth century BC site dedicated to Mefitis, was a Lucanian federal sanctuary.

name: Rotunda of Galerius
description: A brick rotunda of the fourth century AD ordered by the emperor Galerius for use as either a tomb or temple; later incorporated into the Christian Church of Agios Georgios. Listed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1988

name: Roujan
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 15 A2 Roujan

name: Rouvroy-les-Merles
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 11 C3 Rouvroy-les-Merles

name: Rouweyha
description: An ancient place, located in modern Syria, with remains of an ancient basilica, residential buildings, and several tombs. Inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 2011 as part of the "Ancient Villages of Northern Syria."

name: Ruoti
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 45 C3 Ruoti

name: Ruus el-Aslab
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 38 D1 Ruus el-Aslab

name: Ruwwafah
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 83 B4 Ruwwafah

name: S. Agapito
description: S. Agapito, the Palestrina Cathedral also known as the Cattedrale di Sant'Agapito martire, was built atop the ruins of Praeneste's civic forum and its temple. The relics of Saint Agapitus were transferred to this location in 898. The present basilica was consecrated on 16 December 1117 by Pope Pasquale I.

name: S. Agnese
description: A titular church on the Via Nomentana, S. Agnese houses the remains of the eponymous saint. The present church was built by Pope Honorius I in the seventh century AD, on the site of a fourth century basilica built by Constantine I.

name: S. Alessandro
description: The basilica and catacombs of S. Alessandro are located at the seventh milestone of the Via Nomentana.

name: S. Anatolia
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 42 E4 S. Anatolia

name: S. Autonomos, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 52 F3 S. Autonomos, Mon.

name: S. Benedictus
description: A monastery originally established by St. Benedict of Nursia in A.D. 529/530.

name: S. Buono
description: A fortified town near Chieti in the Abruzzo, the ancient phases of S. Buono include evidence for an indigenous sacred site of the later first millennium BC. Among the finds are votive objects dedicated to Herakles.

name: S. Clemens
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 E2 S. Clemens

name: S. Cornelia, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 B1 S. Cornelia, Mon.

name: S. Cristina d’Aro
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 I4 S. Cristina d’Aro

name: S. Cugat
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 H4 S. Cugat

name: S. Desideratus, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 18 B3 S. Desideratus, Mon.

name: S. Eulalia de Bóveda
description: Located some 16 km from Lugo, S. Eulalia de Bóveda is the location of a vaulted, three-aisled, underground building, likely dating to the middle Imperial period and functioning as a nymphaeum.

name: S. Giovanni in Galdo
description: Site of a small Samnitic sanctuary atop Colle Rimontato (709 m. a.s.l.)

name: S. Giovanni Lippioni
description: S. Giovanni Lippioni is the site of a pre-Roman sanctuary that was later repurposed as a Christian church.

name: S. Jorge
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 D3 S. Jorge

name: S. Laurentius
description: The Papal Basilica of Saint Lawrence outside the Walls (Basilica Papale di San Lorenzo fuori le Mura) is one of the Seven Pilgrim Churches of Rome. The basilica serves as the shrine and tomb dedicated to Saint Lawrence and its earliest phases likely belong to the fourth century AD.

name: S. Lidano
description: The so-called "Archi di San Lidano" are the remains of a Roman stone bridge in the Pontine plain, near the modern locality of Sezze Scalo. The bridge likely dates originally to the second century B.C. Evidence of an ancient sanctuary have also been detected in the vicinity.

name: S. Maria d’Artès
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 G4 S. Maria d’Artès

name: S. Maria di Galeria
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 43 B1 S. Maria di Galeria

name: S. Maria di Minerbe
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 39 H2 S. Maria di Minerbe

name: S. Martinus
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 44 E3 S. Martinus

name: S. Pantaleón
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 25 B3 S. Pantaleón

name: S. Passera
description: S. Passera is a Christian church on the Tiber river in Rome, opposite the Basilica of Saint Paul Outside the Walls. The current church dates to the ninth century and incorporated the remains of a Roman tomb. The church is traditionally connected to a community of local miners.

name: S. Paulus
description: One of Rome's four major papal basilicae, S. Paulus was founded by Constantine I in the early fourth century A.D. and expanded by Valentinian I in the 370s. The basilica, with many renovations, repairs, and improvements, stood until the 19th century, when it was destroyed in a fire. The reconstructed basilica only generally resembles the original structure.

name: S. Petrus
description: The Basilica of St. Peter in Vatican City is currently a Renaissance edifice standing on the site of Old St. Peter's Basilica, a church built by Constantine I between AD 319 and 333. The holy site is located on the Mons Vaticanus in connection with the purported site of the burial of the Christian Saint Peter.

name: S. Pietro ad centum muros
description: On the hill of Grignano, located on the outskirts of Montebuono, the remains of a Roman villa were first excavated in the nineteenth century in the area of the church of "S. Pietro ad centum muros". Excavation in the 1990s revealed further villa remains beneath the church and in the vicinity of its cemetery. Epigraphic evidence attesting to the consulship of M. Vipsanius Agrippa was discovered immured in the church (CIL 9.4677 and 9.4779).

name: S. Porphyrius
description: The oldest Orthodox church in Gaza, sacred to the fifth century Saint Porphyrius whose tomb lies within the church.

name: S. Quiricus, Mon.
description: An ancient place, cited: BAtlas 14 I3 S. Quiricus, Mon.

name: S. Salvatore di Cabras
